All right, welcome to your Challengers tracking sheet. So we're gonna go through how to use this sheet so you stay super organized with all of your challenge groups. So the first page that we're looking at here is the breakdown between a classic challenge, which you are doing in your gym with workouts and everything like that. The other option would be hybrid challenges, so people that are not working out at your gym and are just doing hybrid fulfillment or a combination of the two. But this is a way for you to stay organized to know who's who. So basically, this sheet is designed to give you an overview of all of the groups that you have going or past groups, so you can look to see if you are improving performance. So I put in challenge group one here. You can name these whatever you'd like to name them. You can start with one and keep going from there. You can put in the date that they start. However, you want to identify the groups. Then you'll put the weigh in date, so when that entire group starts, the weigh out date, so when that entire group finishes, how many challengers are in that group? So once this challenge is up, after 11, 7, you can put in how many of them continued. So let's say you had 15. It will tell you that your retention is 68%. Okay, so that's all this sheet is for, is just to give you an idea of how many people you have per group and how many are continuing, so you have a good idea of your retention and whether you should fix it or not. So let's say you had poor retention. Let's say you only had seven people continue, and you want to look to see what happened with that group and maybe why they did not continue. So you could click on group one, but before you do that, as you can see group one here is essentially a list of all of the people that are taking that particular challenge, but you want to make a template first. So what you would do is you want to copy this, so you have a blank template here. You want to hit copy. I'm sorry, you just want to hit duplicate, and then you can rename this. Let's just rename this group two. So every time you have a new group, you want to add a new one of these. Do not rename your template or you will lose it. Okay, so we're not gonna use this. We are gonna take a look at group one here. So here you might have the list, or you should have a list of 22 people. If that's how many people were on your challenge group here, then that should be the same amount of people that you have on here. So I only put one for an example, but here you're gonna have all of the details from these people. So what we're looking for particularly is going to be what kind of challenge they're taking, so we could check classic. Just in case you're looking at a group, you don't have to go back to the first page and try to remember. And then here you want to mark the date that they were supposed to weigh in. Did they show? That's a huge indicator of if they're going to continue, if they showed for their weigh in. Same thing with halfway. When was their halfway meeting scheduled for? Or their abandoned home meeting? So we could say halfway through, did they show? Same thing with way out. When is their way out scheduled? Maybe they didn't show for that one, right? So that might be an indicator as to why if people aren't showing up to their way out and you have poor retention on your challenge groups, you might want to make more of an effort to get everybody to continue. Here we are want to track in how many visits. So if they're supposed to be attending three visits per week or reaching out to you three times per week if they're hybrid, here you can mark to see how many times they're doing it. And maybe if somebody is really inconsistent, then you know, hey, for whatever reason, the retouch that you're doing are ineffective because people are not working out or not reaching back to you as frequently as they should be. You'd want to see like three or fours all the way across. So as soon as you do meet with them at the halfway meeting or way out, and they have for sure decided that they're going to continue, you can check off the box that says continuing. Did they purchase supplements? You want to make sure that each one of your challengers purchases supplements, it gets them more bought in and it'll get them better results. So you want to check that off to see if they did that. And then finally, did they take the way out offer? So if you have a way out offer, which is maybe a paid in full or maybe it's an EFT, however you're getting them to continue, did they select that or did they not decide to continue? So if they did, you should be able to drop down and then pick if they purchased a year in advance or if they went into an EFT, right? So that is the detail of the group. You should have 22 people on here. And then the only other page that I want to show you is the list of all challengers. So let's say you get people signed up. You want to just put everybody on this list. So you have a huge collection of all of your active challengers and it'll be as simple as just copying this information when you get to your group. And then you can just paste that information in here. So you don't have to type it twice. So you'll just be pulling these in as they get signed up and you're typing in all of their information. You should be able to pull this off your lead sheet and then put them right in here as they get signed up and just move them to the correct group. And this will keep you super organized. Here you have all of your groups. Take a look at your retention. If it's really good, then make sure that you know what you have done to make it really good, which should be in your history of the groups that they are. If it's really poor, take a look and see if some of these numbers look a little bit off and where you can make improvements so more of your challenge groups continue.
